title: Fee calculation uses unverified external price data without access control
reason: Not reportable as stated: `calculateFee` is view-only, and `updateState` writes state under key `vault = msg.sender`; arbitrary EOAs cannot mutate fee state for real vaults unless they are the vault caller.

title: Signature queues bypass global queue pause mechanism
Transfer whitelist check logic is inverted
Signature queues bypass deposit/redeem fee hooks
Performance fee charged on price drawdowns instead of gains
Permissionless setVault can be initialization-hijacked in non-atomic deployments
reason: Duplicate of existing finding F-006 (with overstated impact).

title: Disallowing subvault asset permanently locks already-held funds
Redeem hook can be griefed by dusting disallowed subvaults
Claim functions lack nonReentrant protection in queues
reason: Incorrect: `RedeemQueue.claim` is `nonReentrant`; `DepositQueue.claim` path does not expose a demonstrated reentrancy drain primitive in current flow.

title: Removing oracle-supported asset breaks queue and risk manager operations
Signature redeem burns shares from arbitrary recipients without authorization
Vault-subvault accounting assumes exact ERC20 transfer amounts
Payable ERC20 queue entrypoints permanently trap accidental ETH
reason: Mostly duplicate of F-019 and partially inaccurate (`RedeemQueue.redeem` is not payable).

title: Consensus threshold can be bypassed with duplicated signer entries
Deposit cancellation uses oracle checkpoint value as Fenwick index
Protocol fee can be minted multiple times per period by reporting non-base assets
Permissionless claiming allows third parties to impose targeted-lockup timing
